name: Lit (Web Components Library)

performance.notes: Tiny (~5KB); faster rendering than React for simple components

agent_readiness.notes: Google's library for building Web Components. @customElement('my-element'). @property() for reactive properties. html`` template literals with tagged templates. @state() for internal state. Shadow DOM encapsulation. css`` for scoped styles. Lifecycle: connectedCallback, updated(). Works in any framework or framework-free. TypeScript decorators. LitElement base class. Used by Adobe, AWS, Salesforce for design systems. Native browser standard — no virtual DOM.

gotchas: Shadow DOM makes styling from outside harder (CSS custom properties needed)
Decorator syntax requires TypeScript or Babel config
Slot-based composition different from React children
best_when: Progressive enhancement on existing HTML pages
avoid_when: Single-page apps needing routing/state management (React/Vue/Svelte better)
Teams already invested in React component ecosystem
When SSR is a primary requirement (limited Lit SSR)
summary: Lit is Google's library for building Web Components on browser standards. @customElement, @property, html`` template literals, css`` scoped styles. ~5KB, no virtual DOM. Works in any framework. Shadow DOM encapsulation. Used by major design systems (Adobe Spectrum, AWS). Framework-agnostic component library foundation. Best when building components that need to work across multiple frameworks.
